sourceNotes: Odisha: Naveen Patnaik CM (2001-2005). Main issues: post-super cyclone recovery, mining and industrial lease allocations, POSCO steel plant MOU, Kalinganagar land dispute clashes, Mahanadi basin flood management. Built programmatically matching the schema and calibration constraints.

newsItems.title: Cyclone Rehabilitation Progress Audited (2001-01)
newsItems.description: Chief Minister Naveen Patnaik orders a comprehensive audit of post-super cyclone shelter construction and relief distribution, vowing transparency and action against corrupt officers.

reactionOptions.text: The state government defends the audit as a critical step to ensure that cyclone aid reaches genuine beneficiaries, addressing the cyclone rehab issues.

risk.badOutcome: Delays in verification camps spark localized protests from coastal villager groups in relation to cyclone rehab.

reactionOptions.text: Opposition Congress leaders claim the audit is a political exercise to delay actual shelter delivery, criticizing the handling of the cyclone rehab developments.

risk.badOutcome: The Congress criticism is ignored as rural voter groups welcome transparency audits regarding the cyclone rehab.

reactionOptions.text: A joint committee of civic representatives and audit teams is formed to monitor district-wise shelter works, seeking a consensus on the cyclone rehab situation.

risk.badOutcome: Audit coordination is delayed by a lack of transport in remote coastal blocks in the cyclone rehab joint efforts.

reactionOptions.text: The Relief Commissioner declines to comment on the volume of unutilized central rehabilitation funds, citing the sensitivity of the cyclone rehab context.

newsItems.title: BJD-BJP Alliance Coordination Meeting Held (2001-02)
newsItems.description: Leaders of the BJD and BJP meet in Bhubaneswar to coordinate municipal poll strategies, reaffirming their coalition program and mutual support across seats.

reactionOptions.text: Alliance leaders welcome the coordination and vow to focus on clean governance targets, addressing the alliance meet issues.

risk.badOutcome: Rebel candidates in several wards threaten to run as independents, causing minor friction in relation to alliance meet.

reactionOptions.text: Opposition Congress leaders claim the BJD-BJP alliance is based on opportunistic power sharing, criticizing the handling of the alliance meet developments.

risk.badOutcome: The Congress claims fail as candidate lists are successfully integrated across divisions regarding the alliance meet.

reactionOptions.text: Both parties agree to establish a joint campaign monitoring desk for municipal elections, seeking a consensus on the alliance meet situation.

risk.badOutcome: Liaison coordination meetings are delayed by a lack of attendance from local district chiefs in the alliance meet joint efforts.

reactionOptions.text: The alliance spokespersons decline to comment on reports of seat sharing friction in Cuttack, citing the sensitivity of the alliance meet context.
